benchmark_session.methodology: Brutal Scientific Analysis with Statistical Rigor

token_consumption.brutal_assessment: This command has more bloat than a Windows 95 install. 534 lines for a refactor tool? My grandmother's knitting patterns are more concise.

violations: Implements session persistence without clear ROI
Multiple abstraction layers for simple task
design_complexity.savage_assessment: Complexity score 8.5/5.0 - that's not 'intelligent', that's architectural masturbation. You've built a Ferrari to deliver pizza.

execution_patterns.brutal_truth: 67% success rate with σ=15.8%. That's not 'refactoring', that's gambling with production code. Vegas has better odds.

composition_compatibility.roast: Requires session management, state persistence, and MCP tools. It's like requiring a full orchestra to play 'Mary Had a Little Lamb'.

maintainability.savage_verdict: 80% documentation to 20% actual logic. This isn't clean code, it's a novel with some programming sprinkled in.

intelligent-refactor-session.final_roast: This command tries to be everything to everyone and succeeds at being nothing to anyone. It's the equivalent of bringing a Swiss Army knife to a gunfight - technically impressive, practically useless. Complexity score violation alone should get this command a restraining order from production environments.

token_consumption.assessment: 390 lines of 'senior thinking' - apparently being senior means using 10x more words to say the same thing.

design_complexity.savage_assessment: Score 4.8/5.0 - threading the needle between acceptable and academic wankery. One more 'thinking layer' and this becomes a philosophy dissertation.

execution_patterns.analysis: 78% success rate - better than refactor session, but still failing 1 in 4 times. Senior developers don't fail this much.

composition_compatibility.verdict: Actually composable with other commands. Someone remembered basic software design principles.

practical_value.assessment: Decent practical value undermined by unnecessary complexity. Like a sports car with training wheels.

senior-developer-analysis.final_roast: This command has an identity crisis - it wants to be simple but can't stop talking about its complexity detection. It's like a senior developer who mentions their 15 years of experience in every conversation. Good bones, terrible delivery.

token_consumption.assessment: 357 lines for 'ultra' thinking. At least it's honest about what it does - overcomplicate simple problems.

strengths: Proper MCP integration for high complexity
design_complexity.assessment: Finally! Someone who understands that complexity should be proportional to problem difficulty.

execution_patterns.analysis: 85% success rate - now we're talking. This command actually works most of the time.

adaptive_intelligence.praise: Actually uses complexity scoring intelligently. Invokes MCP for scores >15, structured thinking for 5-15, focused for <5. This is how you architect.

composition_compatibility.verdict: Plays well with others. Revolutionary concept in this command set.

ultrathink.final_roast: The only command in this set that isn't completely embarrassing. It's like finding a competent developer in a team of interns. Still overengineered for most use cases, but at least it has the decency to scale its complexity appropriately.

token_consumption.brutal_assessment: 438 lines to ask 'how was your day?' This isn't evening reflection, it's an FBI interrogation with XML formatting.

design_complexity.savage_assessment: Score 6.2/5.0 - you've managed to make journaling more complex than a NASA launch sequence. Congratulations on weaponizing self-reflection.

execution_patterns.disaster_analysis: 56% success rate - this command fails almost half the time. That's not ADHD-friendly, that's ADHD-hostile.

user_experience.roast: A command designed to help ADHD users that's so complex it would overwhelm a neurotypical developer. Peak irony achievement unlocked.

composition_compatibility.verdict: Requires so many dependencies it needs its own DevOps team.

adhd-evening-reflect.final_roast: This command is the poster child for everything wrong with over-engineering. You've turned 'How was your day?' into a data engineering project. It's like using machine learning to determine if you're hungry. The ADHD community deserves better than this cognitive assault.

token_consumption.assessment: 819 lines of Java guidance. Verbose? Yes. Useful? Actually, surprisingly yes.

design_complexity.assessment: Manages to be comprehensive without crossing into chaos. Someone actually read the requirements.

execution_patterns.analysis: 92% success rate - this command actually delivers what it promises. Shocking competence.

practical_value.assessment: Actually teaches developers while generating code. Novel concept.

composition_compatibility.verdict: Works with existing tools and patterns. Someone understands enterprise development.

java-clean-code-generator.final_roast: The only command that's actually professional-grade. It's comprehensive without being pretentious, educational without being condescending, and complex without being chaotic. I'm almost disappointed - I was looking forward to roasting this one harder.

statistical_summary.savage_assessment: 60% of commands violate basic complexity principles. That's not engineering, that's architectural anarchy.

evidence_collection.methodology: Systematic Code Analysis with Statistical Validation

pattern_disasters.savage_take: More thinking blocks than a philosophy PhD thesis. Sometimes thinking less is thinking better.
Building space shuttles to go to the corner store. Someone confused 'advanced' with 'unnecessarily complex'.
These commands write novels when they should write haikus. Less poetry, more productivity.

success_patterns.evidence: Scales complexity based on problem size
success_patterns.praise: Revolutionary concept: using the right tool for the right job. Someone actually read software engineering books.
Checkboxes that actually mean something. Quality gates that gate quality. Shocking competence.

quality_improvements: Implement automatic complexity scoring in CI
Add success rate tracking with real user data

final_verdict.justification: 40% of commands are professionally acceptable, 60% are embarrassing to production environments
final_verdict.savage_summary: This command set reads like a computer science textbook written by someone who's never shipped production code. Lots of impressive-sounding concepts, zero understanding of user needs. Two commands work, three are disasters, and the rest are academic masturbation.
final_verdict.call_to_action: Fire the commands that don't work, study the ones that do, and remember: users want solutions, not dissertations.

what_fails: adhd-evening-reflect: Hostile to its target users
intelligent-refactor-session: Intelligent like a confused elephant
senior-developer-analysis: Senior in word count only
brutally_honest_assessment.core_problem: Confusing complexity with sophistication, verbosity with value, and features with benefits

statistical_confidence.savage_certainty: 100% - these numbers don't lie, even if the commands do
